Home / Blog / Preparing for IEB Exams: What Makes Them Different
Exam prep

Preparing for IEB Exams: What Makes Them Different

By the Whiz Tutoring team · 12 May 2026 · 7 min read

IEB and NSC matrics earn the same certificate, but the papers feel different. IEB assessments lean harder on unseen application — using what you know in contexts you haven’t rehearsed.

What actually differs

Expect more multi-step problems in maths, more open-ended analysis in English and history, and science questions that combine topics across the syllabus. Rote memorisation buys you the first 50%; the distinction band is earned on transfer and reasoning.

Term-by-term preparation

Terms 1–2: close every content gap while the pace is manageable, and build the habit of attempting unfamiliar problems without panic. Term 3: prelims are the full dress rehearsal — treat the weeks before them as seriously as finals, then mine the marked papers for patterns in lost marks. Term 4: timed past papers, alternating subjects, with targeted re-drilling of weak question types.

Use the IEB’s own materials

The IEB publishes past papers and assessment matrices; your school’s prelim papers (and other IEB schools’ prelims, which circulate widely) are the closest proxy for the real thing. A tutor who knows the IEB dialect — command words, mark allocation logic — is worth their hour many times over in this system.

Want one-on-one help with this?

Our screened Cape Town tutors cover exactly this — first lesson half price.
